name: Costco Anywhere Visa® Card by Citi
effective_cost: Free — no annual fee, but requires a paid Costco membership ($65/yr or more) to hold the card at all

verdict.text: An excellent card if you're already a paying Costco member and drive regularly — the gas and Costco-purchase rates are hard to beat. If you'd cancel your Costco membership without this card, don't get it for the card alone.

earn_rates.category: Gas at Costco (worldwide) — first $7,000/yr combined with other gas/EV charging, then 1%
Gas & EV charging elsewhere — first $7,000/yr combined with Costco gas, then 1%
Restaurants & eligible travel (airfare, hotels, car rentals, Costco Travel)

earn_note: The 5%/4% gas rate is capped at a combined $7,000/year in Costco + other gas/EV-charging spend (about $350 in rewards), dropping to 1% after that — heavy drivers will hit this cap well before year-end. Requires an active, paid Costco membership to apply for or keep the card; if your membership lapses, so does the card. No annual cap on the 3% or 2% categories found.

redemption_options.method: Annual reward certificate, issued once your February billing statement closes — redeemable for cash or merchandise at any U.S. Costco warehouse (incl. Puerto Rico)

points.per_100k: Not applicable — this is a cash-back card with no points currency; a $1,000-equivalent year of 2–5% earning becomes a roughly $20–50 certificate depending on category mix
points.note: Unlike most cash-back cards, rewards aren't a running statement credit — they accrue all year and pay out once annually as a single certificate you have to physically redeem in a Costco warehouse (or online per Costco's current process). If you close the account before the certificate is issued, you can lose accrued rewards, so time closures carefully.

transfer_partners.highlight: No transfer partners — straight cash back with no points currency to move anywhere.

insurance.detail: Damage & theft coverage · 120 days (90 days for NY residents) — per-item/per-year caps not published on Citi's public page
Worldwide Car Rental Insurance when you decline the rental company's own collision coverage — secondary/primary status not confirmed
Named benefit — dollar limit not published on Citi's public page

protection_note: A standard Visa (not Visa Signature) no-fee card's protection suite: purchase protection, rental car coverage, and travel accident insurance are named benefits, but Citi's public pages don't publish the exact dollar caps — check your Guide to Benefits for specifics. No trip cancellation/interruption, baggage delay, or cell phone protection, so this isn't a substitute for a dedicated travel card on bigger trips.
rental_note: Worldwide Car Rental Insurance applies when you decline the counter's own collision/theft coverage and charge the full rental to this card; Citi's public page doesn't confirm whether it pays primary or secondary to your personal auto policy.

services.name: No foreign transaction fees
services.detail: $0 on purchases made outside the US.

services.name: Doubles as Costco membership ID
services.detail: The card can be used to verify membership at warehouse entry while the membership is active.

services.name: Roadside assistance dispatch
services.detail: Pay-per-use dispatch service available in the US, Puerto Rico, and US Virgin Islands.

services.name: Free FICO Score access
services.detail: Monthly FICO Score available via Citi's online account and app.

services.name: No fee for additional cards
services.detail: Authorized users can be added at no extra cost.

additional_cards.title: Authorized users

benefits.text: Earns cash back on their spend (into your account)
Same purchase/rental protections as the primary card

additional_cards.note: Free to add — a natural fit for a household member who also shops at Costco, since all spend rolls into one annual certificate.

timeline.text: Card eligibility and continued use are tied to holding a paid Costco membership; this has been the structure since Citi took over the Costco co-brand portfolio from American Express in 2016.
